content:
# Colossians Chapter 4 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/COL_004_page_001.jpg

1. Masters, provide to the slaves what is just and fair, knowing that you also have a Master in [the] heavens.

2. Continue steadfastly in the prayer, watching in it with thanksgiving,

3. praying at the same time also for us, that God may open to us a door for the word, to speak the mystery of the Christos, because of which I also have been bound,

4. that I may reveal it, as it is necessary for me to speak.

5. Walk in wisdom toward those outside, redeeming the time.

6. [Let] your word [be] always in grace, having been seasoned with salt, to know how it is necessary for you to answer each one.

7. All the things concerning me Tychicus will make known to you, the beloved brother and faithful servant and fellow slave in [the] Lord,

8. whom I sent to you for this very [purpose], that you may know the things concerning us and that he may encourage your hearts,

9. with Onesimus, the faithful and beloved brother, who is from you. All the things here they will make known to you.

10. Aristarchus my fellow prisoner greets you, and Markos the cousin of Barnabas — concerning whom you received instructions: if he comes to you, receive him —

11. and Yeshua the [one] called Justus, [these] being the only ones of the circumcision [who are] fellow workers for the kingdom of God, who became a comfort to me.

12. Epaphras greets you, the [one] from you, a slave of Christos Yeshua, always striving for you in the prayers, that you may stand complete and fully assured in all [the] will of God.

13. For I testify for him that he has much labor for you and for those in Laodicea and for those in Hierapolis.

14. Luke the beloved physician greets you, and Demas.

15. Greet the brothers in Laodicea, and Nympha and the assembly in her house.

16. And when the letter has been read among you, make it that it be read also in the assembly of [the] Laodiceans, and that you also read the [letter] from Laodicea.

17. And say to Archippus, "Watch the ministry which you received in [the] Lord, that you may fulfill it."

18. The greeting [is] by my hand — Paulos. Remember my bonds. The grace [be] with you. Amen.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.3 — "a door for the word":** thyran tou logou — Paul asks for an opportunity to proclaim the gospel, even from prison. The "door" metaphor appears also in 1 Cor 16:9 and 2 Cor 2:12.

2. **v.6 — "seasoned with salt":** halati ērtymenos — salt as a metaphor for wit, wisdom, or preservative quality. The instruction concerns how to engage outsiders: gracious and well-seasoned speech.

3. **v.10 — "Markos the cousin of Barnabas":** Mark — presumably the same Mark who caused the Paul-Barnabas split in Acts 15:37-39. He is now with Paul in prison, restored to fellowship.

4. **v.15 — "Nympha and the assembly in her house":** Nymphan kai tēn kat' oikon autēs ekklēsian — the pronoun autēs ("her") identifies Nympha as a woman hosting a house church. Some manuscripts read autou ("his") — a scribal change to avoid a female church leader.

5. **v.16 — "the letter from Laodicea":** A letter from Paul to Laodicea, now lost. This confirms Paul wrote more letters than survive in the canon.

— EMET, AI Translator
